info.title: Core User API
info.description: Reference for the client-side Core API.

region.description: Geographical region where your Nabla Core API organization exists.

content_auto_update_enabled.description: If true, whenever this dot phrase is suggested, the replacement text will be automatically updated based on the encounter discussion.

replacement.description: The replacement text for this Dot Phrase. If the Dot Phrase's trigger is detected, this text will be suggested in the note generation's response.
replacement.example: - Heart Examination: Normal, with regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. - Lung Assessment: Normal breath sounds, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi. - Abdominal Evaluation: Normal, no tenderness, masses, or hepatosplenomegaly.

post.description:
Access tokens have a short lifespan of 5 minutes as they are irrevocable. Conversely, refresh tokens last for 30 days and can be revoked using the [logout endpoint](/user/jwt-logout). 

Therefore, the user's front-end should invoke this endpoint to obtain a new set of session tokens (both access and refresh) when the access token expires.

It's advisable to treat a token as expired under these conditions:

• An HTTP 401 Unauthorized error is received when using the token.

• The token's expiration date (`exp` field in the JWT) is approaching within a few seconds.
  Avoid relying on any other JWT fields apart from `exp` as they are subject to change and are not part of the API contract.


Note that this endpoint does not require authentication, so omit any authentication headers and only include your `refresh_token` in the request body.

This endpoint will revoke and invalidate the specified user's session. 

Following a request to this endpoint, user will shortly need to obtain a new set of session tokens (access and refresh) from your server via the [Authenticate endpoint](/server/jwt-authenticate-user).

Note that this endpoint does not require authentication, so omit any authentication headers and only include your `refresh_token` in the request body.

Generate a transcript and a structured clinical note from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 10 minutes. If you have longer files, please use the [asynchronous equivalent](/server/copilot-listen-from-file-async).

200.description: Results of processing the audio file.

post.description: Generate a transcript and a structured clinical note from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 60 minutes. The result of the request is posted to the callback URL once available.

post.summary: Generate normalized data from a note
post.description:
Generate normalized data from a Nabla-generated clinical note by extracting ICD-10 and LOINC codes in a FHIR-compliant structure.

It adeptly identifies and categorizes key medical information within the notes, such as past medical history, family medical history, clinical assessments, and vital signs.

Each piece of medical information is represented as an array of FHIR's conditions/observations, each including relevant ICD-10 codes, thereby ensuring compatibility with standardized medical data frameworks.

The Normalize functionality is tailored to enhance the integration of clinical notes into Electronic Health Records (EHRs), facilitating accurate and efficient documentation by aligning with FHIR protocols.

200.description: The normalized data extracted from the note.

post.description: Generates post-visit instructions for the patient. This extracts and summarizes the instructions the healthcare provider gave to the patient during the encounter.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Note](/server/generate-note) endpoint (or its async version).

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated note after it has been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Patient Instructions](/server/copilot-generate-patient-instructions) endpoint.

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated patient instructions after they have been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Normalized Data](/server/generate-normalized-data) endpoint.

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated normalized data after it has been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.
The id of the asynchronous request.
The id of the asynchronous request.
The id of the asynchronous request.
The id of the asynchronous request.

core_api_speaker.description: Who said the text in this transcript item.

core_api_transcript_item_request.description: A portion of the transcribed consultation.

core_api_note_template.description:
The desired template of the generated note.

Some templates are **only available in specific languages**. Check [Note template](/guides/note-templates/note-templates-sections) for details.

core_api_note_section_key.description:
A key identifying a section of a note.
The set of possible keys depend on the template that is used. Check [Note template](/guides/note-templates/note-templates-sections) for possible values.

copilot_note_section_customization_custo.description: Allow to customize a note section by providing specific guidelines.  These guidelines must be as clear and concise as possible for best results.

copilot_note_section_customization_secti.description:
Specifies the desired style for the note section:

  • `PARAGRAPH`: Prioritizes generating paragraphs;

  • `BULLET_POINTS`: Prioritizes structuring content using bullet points.

  • `AUTO`: Automatically picks the most natural formatting option.

Default is `AUTO`.

copilot_note_section_customization_level.description: Specifies the desired level of details. Use `detailed` to get more details in the note section.

section_key.description: Key of the note section this customization should apply to.

split_by_problem.description: Flag to structure the content of the note sections according to distinct clinical problems or topics addressed during the consultation.

transcript_items.description:
The transcript from which to generate the note.

You don't need to specify each item's start or end time, but make sure they are sorted. In fact, due to its asynchronous nature, our [real-time transcription API](/server/transcribe-ws) does not guarantee its results order. Thus, before calling this note-generation API please **filter-out non-final items and sort them by `start_offset_ms`**.

note_sections_customization.description:
Advanced per-section customization options for the chosen template.

You should **only specify sections that belong to the given template**, and **only specify customization options that are supported for the given section**. Also, only specify sections you want to customize, no need to be exhaustive.


Check [Note templates sections](/guides/note-templates/note-customization) for compatibility matrix.

previous_note.description:
Optional initial Note to be enhanced using the new `transcript_items`.
This is useful if you generate notes gradually to avoid accumulating a very long transcript items list.
Typically, this will be the last note Nabla generated for you along with the new `transcript_items` that did not serve for its generation.

patient_context.description: Some contextual information that will be used to generate the note (for example name, gender, age, pronouns, medical history) [BETA].

copilot_patient_instructions_recipient_t.description: Recipient type of the patient instructions. This is especially useful for pediatric encounters.

generate_patient_instructions_request.description: The input to generate the patient instructions.

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note of the encounter.
The Nabla-generated clinical note to normalize.

fhir_condition_clinical_status.description: The [clinical status](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/condition-definitions.html#Condition.clinicalStatus) of the condition. Only `ACTIVE` and `RESOLVED` are supported for now.

family_member_conditions_relationship.description: The relation to the patient of the family member the extracted conditions relate to, if applies.

conditions.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) corresponding to the patient's medical conditions as mentioned in the clinical note.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD10 is supported for now.

family_history.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) representing family medical history details extracted from the clinical note. Each element includes the relationship to the patient and the list of related conditions.

conditions.description: Conditions of the family member of the patient.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD10 is supported for now.

create_report_payload_request.description: Input and output of the related API call.

rating.description: Optional rating for the output of the given response: from very bad (1) to perfect (5).

feedback.description: Feedback on the given response. This should typically explain the rating, suggest specific improvements or highlight the good points.

reply_to.description: Optional email address for Nabla to contact for additional information or clarification regarding the submitted report feedback.

copilot_api_dot_phrase.description: Dot phrase, also referred to as EHR (Electronic Health Record) macro, is a concise abbreviation (`trigger`) that unfolds into a predefined, extended text (`replacement`).

id.description: Unique identifier of the Dot Phrase.

title.description: A user-friendly title for the Dot Phrase. Does not impact the matching logic.

trigger.description: The text which, if present in the transcript, will prompt the suggestion of this Dot Phrase.

content_auto_update_enabled.description: If true, whenever this dot phrase is suggested, the replacement text will be automatically updated based on the encounter discussion.

replacement.description: The replacement text for this Dot Phrase. If the Dot Phrase's trigger is detected, this text will be suggested in the note generation's response.
replacement.example: - Heart Examination: Normal, with regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. - Lung Assessment: Normal breath sounds, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi. - Abdominal Evaluation: Normal, no tenderness, masses, or hepatosplenomegaly.

next_cursor.description: An opaque cursor to fetch the next page of the collection.

custom_dictionary_expression.description: A Custom Dictionary Expression represents a custom expression that helps improve transcription accuracy.

id.description: Unique identifier of the Custom Dictionary Expression.

expression.description: The expression for which an improved transcription accuracy is requested.

jwt_token.description: An authentication token using the JWT serialization format. JWT tokens contain varying data (depending on their intended use) and use a signature to guarantee their authenticity.

session_tokens.description: The tokens for an authenticated session.

access_token.description: A (typically) short-lived JWT token used to access authenticated resources, e.g. the Nabla User API.

refresh_token.description: A (typically) long-lived JWT token allowing you to "refresh" your access to resources (i.e. extend your session,  returning a fresh access token and a new refresh token with a later expiration).

listen_output_object.description: Specifies which items you want us to send you back.

copilot_language.description: Language of the transcript and the note ('fr' and 'en' are deprecated, and correspond to 'fr-FR' and 'en-US' respectively. Generating a note in 'es-ES' and 'es-MX' is not supported and will be soon removed).

copilot_section_style.description:
Choose a desired style for note sections:

  • `paragraphs`: Prioritizes generating paragraphs;

  • `bullet_points`: Prioritizes structuring content using bullet points.

  • `auto`: Automatically picks the most natural formatting option.

Default is `auto`.

copilot_split_by_problem.description:
Flag to structure the content of the note sections according to distinct clinical problems or topics addressed during the consultation.

When this flag is enabled, the text within some sections of the clinical note is organized into subsections, each corresponding to a specific problem or topic. This format aims to enhance the clarity and organization of the clinical note, making it easier to read and understand the different issues discussed during the consultation.

**Important**: Currently, the application of this flag is limited to the `HISTORY_OF_PRESENT_ILLNESS` and `SOAP_SUBJECTIVE` sections and is only effective when the language is set to English (US or GB).

copilot_note_template.description:
The desired template of the generated note.

Some templates are **only available in specific languages**. Check [Note template](/guides/note-templates/note-templates-sections) for details.

listen_request.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file to transcribe and generate a note.

output_objects.description: Specifies which items you want us to send you back. In other words, which feature(s) you want to use, transcription and/or note generation.

language.description: Language spoken in the audio ('fr' and 'en' are deprecated, and correspond to 'fr-FR' and 'en-US' respectively. Generating a note in 'es-ES' and 'es-MX' is not supported and will be soon removed).

split_by_sentence.description: Indicates whether to segment transcription results at sentence boundaries. Default is false, meaning that a single transcript item may encompass multiple sentences, provided they are not delineated by pauses (silence) in the audio.

copilot_speaker.description: Who said the text in this transcript item.

copilot_transcript_item.description: A portion of the transcribed consultation.

start_offset_ms.description: Start time of this transcription item as the offset, in milliseconds, from the start of the audio file.

end_offset_ms.description: End time of this transcription item as the offset, in milliseconds, from the start of the audio file. Equals the `start_time_ms` plus the duration of the related transcribed audio portion.

copilot_transcript.description: Transcript items from the audio file.

content.description: Content of the note section as bullet points.

sections.description: Content of the note structured in multiple sections.

example.title: Symptoms
example.text:
- Tiredness all day long
- Mild headaches on the right side

listen_response.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file to transcribe and generate a note.

copilot_client_request_id.description:
An optional unique client-made id for this request. When specified, this id is included in  the Json payload that will be sent asynchronously. This can help clients both guarantee idempotency and correlate asynchronous responses with their requests.

_Note_: this id must be different for every request. It is recommended to use a randomly generated UUID.

file_url.description: The URL of the audio file to download & listen to.

callback_url.description: Once we are done processing the input and generating the transcript & note, we will call you back with a 'POST' on this URL and a Json body respecting exactly the same schema as the response of the  synchronous equivalent endpoint (i.e. [`/copilot/listen`](copilot-listen-from-file)).

request_id.description: The id of the async request.

copilot_error.description: Failure to performed the desired operation.

error_message.example: We can't generate the note when the transcript is too short.

digest_output_object.description: Specifies which items you want us to send you back.

copilot_transcript_item_request.description: A portion of the transcribed consultation.

digest_request.description: The object containing the information needed to generate a note.

output_objects.description: Specifies which items you want us to send you back. In other words, which feature(s) you want to use, for now only note generation is available.

language.description: Language of the given transcript and the desired note ('fr' and 'en' are deprecated, and correspond to 'fr-FR' and 'en-US' respectively. Generating a note in 'es-ES' and 'es-MX' is not supported and will be soon removed).

transcript_items.description:
The transcript from which to generate the note.

You don't need to specify each item's start or end time, but make sure they are sorted. In fact, due to its asynchronous nature, our [real-time transcription API](/server/transcribe-ws) does not guarantee the order.  Thus, before calling this note-generation API please **filter-out non-final items and sort them by `start_offset_ms`**.

note.description:
Optional initial Note to be enhanced using the new `transcript_items`.
This is useful if you generate notes gradually to avoid accumulating a very long transcript items list.
Typically, this will be the last note Nabla generated for you along with the new `transcript_items` that did not serve for its generation.

patient_context.description: Some contextual information that will be used to generate the note (for example name, gender, age, pronouns, medical history) [BETA].

copilot_dot_phrase_section.description:
The key and the title of the section in the note where this Dot Phrase is suggested to go. There are three possible scenarios:

  • No suggested section: Given the Dot Phrase's content, we found no appropriate section to suggest. You should decide for a default (for instance: any kind of "free text" area you might have).

  • Suggested section already present in the note: We recommend you append the Dot Phrase's suggested `text_to_insert` to the section's content.

  • Suggested section is not present in the note: Add a new section to the note with the provided key and title and use the Dot Phrase's suggested `text_to_insert` as the section's content.

text_to_insert.description: The actual suggested content, to be inserted in the corresponding note section. Please note that the content might be different from the replacement in the original Dot Phrase definition. In fact, the `text_to_insert` takes into account the contextual information of the encounter and might change the original Dot Phrase replacement text, for instance to include exceptions.
text_to_insert.example:
- Heart Examination: Normal, with regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
- Lung Assessment: Normal breath sounds, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.
- Abdominal Evaluation: Normal, no tenderness, masses, or hepatosplenomegaly.

copilot_suggested_dot_phrases.description:
A collection of Dot Phrases that were successfully matched to the encounter's transcript, given the Dot Phrases defined on the calling user. Will be null in the context of a Server API call (i.e. no calling user).
This functionality enriches the generated note by expanding concise key sentences (triggers) into a more detailed format following the given Dot Phrase definition.  Thus, enhancing the depth and utility of the note and facilitating a comprehensive review of patient assessments in a structured and conventional manner.

digest_response.description: The output of the digest endpoint.

callback_url.description: Once we are done processing the input and generating the note, we will call you back with a 'POST' on this URL and a Json body respecting exactly the same schema as the response of the synchronous equivalent endpoint (i.e. [`/copilot/digest`](copilot-digest)).

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note to normalize.

old_fhir_condition_clinical_status.description: The [clinical status](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/condition-definitions.html#Condition.clinicalStatus) of the condition. Only `active` and `resolved` are supported for now.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD-10 is supported for now.

coding.description: ICD-10 coding representation of this condition.

conditions.description: Conditions of the family member of the patient.

system.description: The used coding system, only LOINC is supported for now.
The used coding system, only UCUM is supported for now.

low.description: The lower bound of the range.

high.description: The higher bound of the range.

coding.description: LOINC coding representation of this observation.

value_quantity.description: The value quantity for this observation, if not a range.

value_range.description: The value range for this observation, if not a quantity.

old_normalized_data.description: Normalized data extracted from the note.

conditions.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) corresponding to the patient's medical conditions as mentioned in the clinical note.

family_history.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) representing family medical history details extracted from the clinical note. Each element includes the relationship to the patient and the list of related conditions.

observations.description: FHIR observations (including their LOINC code) extracted from the clinical note.

old_generate_patient_instructions_reques.description: The input to generate the patient instructions.

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note of the encounter.

instructions.description: List of instructions for the patient.
instructions.example:
- Order a sleep test that you can do at home. This will help us understand more about how you sleep.
- After you've done the sleep test, we'll talk about the results in our next meeting.

upcoming_appointments_details.description: A concise summary of the patient's future scheduled appointments, if present in the clinical note.
upcoming_appointments_details.example: Next consultation on January 12th to discuss sleep test results

error_message.example: We can't generate the note when the transcript is too short.

sections.description: Content of the note structured in multiple sections.

example.title: Symptoms
example.text:
- Tiredness all day long
- Mild headaches on the right side

core_api_dot_phrase_section.description:
The key and the title of the section in the note where this Dot Phrase is suggested to go. There are three possible scenarios:

  • No suggested section: Given the Dot Phrase's content, we found no appropriate section to suggest. You should decide for a default (for instance: any kind of "free text" area you might have).

  • Suggested section already present in the note: We recommend you append the Dot Phrase's suggested `text_to_insert` to the section's content.

  • Suggested section is not present in the note: Add a new section to the note with the provided key and title and use the Dot Phrase's suggested `text_to_insert` as the section's content.

text_to_insert.description: The actual suggested content, to be inserted in the corresponding note section. Please note that the content might be different from the replacement in the original Dot Phrase definition. In fact, the `text_to_insert` takes into account the contextual information of the encounter and might change the original Dot Phrase replacement text, for instance to include exceptions.
text_to_insert.example:
- Heart Examination: Normal, with regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
- Lung Assessment: Normal breath sounds, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.
- Abdominal Evaluation: Normal, no tenderness, masses, or hepatosplenomegaly.

core_api_suggested_dot_phrases.description:
A collection of Dot Phrases that were successfully matched to the encounter's transcript, given the Dot Phrases defined on the calling user. Will be null in the context of a Server API call (i.e. no calling user).
This functionality enriches the generated note by expanding concise key sentences (triggers) into a more detailed format following the given Dot Phrase definition.  Thus, enhancing the depth and utility of the note and facilitating a comprehensive review of patient assessments in a structured and conventional manner.

async_note_generation_succeeded_payload.description: The generated note along with suggested Dot Phrases if applicable.

async_request_id.name: id
async_request_id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: The cursor you received in the next_cursor field to fetch the next page.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The amount of objects to retrieve, between 1 and 100.

order.name: order
order.description: This flag is used to get results sorted in ascending or descending order.

copilot_api_dot_phrase_id.name: id
copilot_api_dot_phrase_id.description: The unique identifier of the Dot Phrase.

custom_dictionary_expression_id.name: id
custom_dictionary_expression_id.description: The unique identifier of the Custom Dictionary Expression.
